ucs.title: [LLM] The White Company / Operation Shaheen — Office exploit child drops payload then self-deletes (CVE-2012-0158 / Equation Editor chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Operation Shaheen kill-chain attributed to The White Company (G0089): a spearphishing Word/Excel/RTF (often abusing CVE-2012-0158 via EQNEDT32.EXE) detonates, spawns a scripting/LOLBin child, drops a PE/script into %TEMP%/%APPDATA%/%PROGRAMDATA%, then issues a self-delete (cmd /c del, timeout-then-del, ping-then-del) to wipe the dropper — combining T1566.001 → T1203 → T1204.002 → T1070.004 in one chain that is unusually specific to this actor's tooling against Pakistani gov/mil targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval is_selfdel=if(match(process,"(?i)(cmd(\.exe)?\s+/[cC]\s+(timeout|ping|choice).+\s+del\s+|cmd(\.exe)?\s+/[cC]\s+del\s+/[fFqQ].+\.(exe|dll|bat|scr|tmp|rtf|doc|docx|xls|xlsx|ppt))"),1,0)
| join type=inner dest process_id max=0 [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Public\\*")) AND Filesystem.action="created" AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.exe","*.dll","*.bat","*.scr","*.tmp","*.vbs","*.js") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
]
| where is_selfdel=1 OR like(process,"%timeout%") OR like(process,"%ping -n%127.0.0.1%")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process file_path file_name
ucs.kql:
// The White Company / Operation Shaheen exploit-to-self-delete chain
let Lookback = 14d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let ExecChildren  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let DropPaths     = dynamic([@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\"]);
// 1. Office app spawned a scripting/LOLBin child (T1203 / T1204.002)
let OfficeChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
    | where FileName in~ (ExecChildren)
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              OfficeParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildName = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ChildPid = ProcessId,
              ChildSHA256 = SHA256;
// 2. That child wrote a payload into a known stager path within 5 minutes (T1027.002 dropped artefact)
let PayloadDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (DropPaths)
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".bat"
         or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".vbs" or FileName endswith ".js"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              DroppedPath = FolderPath, DroppedFile = FileName, DroppedSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperPid = InitiatingProcessId, DropperName = InitiatingProcessFileName;
let ExploitChain = OfficeChildren
    | join kind=inner PayloadDrops on DeviceId
    | where DropTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 5m)
    | where DropperName in~ (ExecChildren) or DropperPid == ChildPid;
// 3. Self-delete invocation observed on the same device shortly after the drop (T1070.004 — Shaheen signature)
let SelfDelete = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where FileName =~ "cmd.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(/c|/r)\s+(timeout(\s+/t)?\s+\d+|ping(\s+-n)?\s+\d+\s+127\.0\.0\.1|choice\s+/c\s+y\s+/n\s+/d\s+y\s+/t\s+\d+)?[^|]*\bdel\b.*\.(exe|dll|bat|scr|vbs|js|tmp|rtf|doc[xm]?|xls[xm]?|ppt[xm]?)"
    | project DelTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DelCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              DelParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, DelParentPid = InitiatingProcessId;
ExploitChain
| join kind=inner SelfDelete on DeviceId
| where DelTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 10m)
| project SpawnTime, DropTime, DelTime,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent, OfficeParentCmd,
          ChildName, ChildCmd,
          DroppedPath, DroppedFile, DroppedSHA256,
          SelfDeleteCmd = DelCmd
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// The White Company / Operation Shaheen exploit-to-self-delete chain (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let Lookback = 14d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe"]);
let ExecChildren  = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let OfficeChildren = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
    | where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents) and ChildLeaf in (ExecChildren)
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              OfficeParent = ParentLeaf, ChildName = ChildLeaf,
              ChildCmd = CommandLine, ChildPid = NewProcessId;
let SelfDelete = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(/c|/r)\s+(timeout(\s+/t)?\s+\d+|ping(\s+-n)?\s+\d+\s+127\.0\.0\.1|choice\s+/c\s+y\s+/n\s+/d\s+y\s+/t\s+\d+)?[^|]*\bdel\b.*\.(exe|dll|bat|scr|vbs|js|tmp|rtf|doc[xm]?|xls[xm]?|ppt[xm]?)"
    | project DelTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, DelCmd = CommandLine,
              DelParent = ParentProcessName;
OfficeChildren
| join kind=inner SelfDelete on Computer
| where DelTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 10m)
| project SpawnTime, DelTime, Computer, Account,
          OfficeParent, ChildName, ChildCmd, SelfDeleteCmd = DelCmd
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: The White Company's Operation Shaheen lures (Cylance, Nov 2018) chain CVE-2012-0158 / Equation Editor exploitation in spearphishing attachments to a dropper that writes its second-stage to a user-writable path and immediately wipes itself with a `cmd /c (timeout|ping) ... && del /f /q ...` self-delete — a sequence Cylance specifically called out as a Shaheen signature. Filtering for Office-app par

ucs.title: [LLM] The White Company — AV product enumeration from Office-spawned child (Operation Shaheen anti-detection branding logic)
ucs.description: Surfaces the unusual Cylance-documented behaviour where The White Company implants enumerate installed AV products (Sophos, Kaspersky, ESET, BitDefender, AVG, Avira, Quick Heal, Trend Micro) via WMI SecurityCenter2 / registry queries to deliberately surrender execution to specific AVs as an attribution-muddying tactic — when this AV-discovery activity is parented by an Office app or recent dropper, it is highly characteristic of Operation Shaheen and not random T1518.001 noise.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","reg.exe","cmd.exe") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval is_av_query=case(
    match(process,"(?i)SecurityCenter2|AntiVirusProduct|AntiSpywareProduct"),1,
    match(process,"(?i)Get-?WmiObject\s+.*Antivirus"),1,
    match(process,"(?i)\\\\SOFTWARE\\\\(Sophos|ESET|Kaspersky|BitDefender|AVG|Avira|Quick\s*Heal|TrendMicro|Symantec|McAfee|Avast)"),1,
    match(process,"(?i)\\b(sophos|eset|kavfs|kavsvc|avp|bdservicehost|avgnt|avgsvc|quhlpsvc|tmlisten|ntrtscan|mcshield|mfemms)\\b"),1,
    1=0,0)
| eval is_time_check=if(match(process,"(?i)\\b(GetSystemTime|w32tm|net\\s+time|systeminfo|Get-Date)\\b"),1,0)
| where is_av_query=1 OR is_time_check=1
| stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(process_name) as cmds_used values(process) as cmdlines sum(is_av_query) as av_query_count sum(is_time_check) as time_check_count by dest user parent_process_name
| where av_query_count>=1 AND parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe")
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// The White Company — AV-vendor enumeration parented by Office/script LOLBin (Shaheen anti-detection logic)
let Lookback = 14d;
let ShaheenParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let AvVendorTokens = dynamic(["sophos","eset","kaspersky","bitdefender","avg","avira","quick heal","quickheal","trendmicro","trend micro","symantec","mcafee","avast","f-secure"]);
let AvProcessTokens = dynamic(["savservice","sophosui","ekrn","egui","avp.exe","kavfs","bdservicehost","vsserv","avgnt.exe","avgsvc","avguard","quhlpsvc","qhpisvr","tmlisten","ntrtscan","mcshield","mfemms","avastsvc","fsav"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ShaheenParents)
| where FileName in~ ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","reg.exe","cmd.exe","tasklist.exe","sc.exe")
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend HitsSecurityCenter2 = cmd has_any ("securitycenter2","antivirusproduct","antispywareproduct")
| extend HitsGetWmiObjectAv  = cmd matches regex @"get-?wmiobject\s+[^|]*antivirus"
           or cmd matches regex @"get-?ciminstance\s+[^|]*antivirus"
| extend HitsRegAvKey        = cmd matches regex @"software\\(sophos|eset|kaspersky|bitdefender|avg|avira|quick\s*heal|trendmicro|symantec|mcafee|avast|f-secure)"
| extend HitsAvProcEnum      = cmd has_any (AvProcessTokens) and FileName in~ ("tasklist.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| extend HitsVendorString    = cmd has_any (AvVendorTokens)
| where HitsSecurityCenter2 or HitsGetWmiObjectAv or HitsRegAvKey or HitsAvProcEnum or HitsVendorString
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          OfficeParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          EnumTool = FileName,
          EnumCmd  = ProcessCommandLine,
          HitsSecurityCenter2, HitsGetWmiObjectAv, HitsRegAvKey, HitsAvProcEnum, HitsVendorString
| summarize EnumEventCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            Tools = make_set(EnumTool, 10),
            Cmds  = make_set(EnumCmd, 10),
            HitVendorRegistry = max(toint(HitsRegAvKey)),
            HitSecCenter2     = max(toint(HitsSecurityCenter2))
            by DeviceName, AccountName, OfficeParent
| where EnumEventCount >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// The White Company — AV-vendor enumeration parented by Office/script LOLBin (Sentinel 4688)
let Lookback = 14d;
let ShaheenParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let AvVendorTokens = dynamic(["sophos","eset","kaspersky","bitdefender","avg","avira","quick heal","quickheal","trendmicro","trend micro","symantec","mcafee","avast","f-secure"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (ShaheenParents)
| where ChildLeaf in ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","reg.exe","cmd.exe","tasklist.exe","sc.exe")
| extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| extend HitsSecurityCenter2 = cmd has_any ("securitycenter2","antivirusproduct","antispywareproduct")
| extend HitsGetWmiObjectAv  = cmd matches regex @"get-?wmiobject\s+[^|]*antivirus"
           or cmd matches regex @"get-?ciminstance\s+[^|]*antivirus"
| extend HitsRegAvKey        = cmd matches regex @"software\\(sophos|eset|kaspersky|bitdefender|avg|avira|quick\s*heal|trendmicro|symantec|mcafee|avast|f-secure)"
| extend HitsVendorString    = cmd has_any (AvVendorTokens)
| where HitsSecurityCenter2 or HitsGetWmiObjectAv or HitsRegAvKey or HitsVendorString
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentLeaf, ChildLeaf, CommandLine,
          HitsSecurityCenter2, HitsGetWmiObjectAv, HitsRegAvKey, HitsVendorString
| summarize EnumEventCount = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            Tools = make_set(ChildLeaf, 10),
            Cmds  = make_set(CommandLine, 10)
            by Computer, Account, ParentLeaf
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: AV Vendor Enumeration Spawned by Office or Script Host (The White Company / Operation Shaheen)
id: 8c3a6f1e-7a2c-4d83-9b7a-tw0c0g0089001
status: experimental
description: Detects AV-product reconnaissance (WMI SecurityCenter2, registry vendor keys, Get-WmiObject AntiVirusProduct) launched by an Office application or script-host parent — characteristic of The White Company's Operation Shaheen branding/anti-detection logic against Sophos/ESET/Kaspersky/BitDefender/AVG/Avira/Quick Heal/Trend Micro.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0089/
  - https://www.cylance.com/content/dam/cylance/pdfs/reports/Operation_Shaheen.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1518.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - g0089
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_office_or_script:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  enum_tool:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
  av_recon_indicators:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'SecurityCenter2'
      - 'AntiVirusProduct'
      - 'AntiSpywareProduct'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\Sophos'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\ESET'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\Kaspersky'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\BitDefender'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\AVG'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\Avira'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\Quick Heal'
      - 'SOFTWARE\\TrendMicro'
  condition: parent_office_or_script and enum_tool and av_recon_indicators
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned IT inventory or compliance scripts pushed via management tooling (rare from Office parents)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Cylance's Operation Shaheen report (Nov 2018) is explicit that The White Company's implants enumerate installed AV products via SecurityCenter2 WMI and SOFTWARE\<Vendor> registry paths, then deliberately allow detection by certain Pakistani-prevalent AVs (Sophos, ESET, Kaspersky, BitDefender, AVG, Avira, Quick Heal, Trend Micro) — a near-unique 'branding' / counter-attribution behaviour. Generic T
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
